answer:
**Viṣṇu Smṛti, Chapter 44**, lists the karmic consequences of theft in terms of animal rebirths, matching the query precisely across verses 14–29 (and beyond).[^1]

Here are the relevant verses:

> A grain-thief becomes a rat. // Vi_44.14 //[^2]  
> A stealer of bronze becomes a swan. // Vi_44.15 //[^3]  
> Of milk, a crow. // Vi_44.18 //[^4]  
> Of clarified butter, an ichneumon [mongoose]. // Vi_44.20 //[^5]  
> Of meat, a vulture. // Vi_44.21 //[^6]  
> Of salt, a cricket. // Vi_44.24 //[^7]  
> Stealing silk, one becomes a partridge. // Vi_44.26 //[^8]  
> A cow, an iguana. // Vi_44.29 //[^9]

(A near-identical list appears in **Manusmṛti, Chapter 12**, verses 62–67, e.g., Mn_12.62: "Having stolen grain, one becomes a rat; bronze, a swan... milk, a crow... ghee, a mongoose," etc.[^10] Vishnu's chapter, however, dedicates itself entirely to such animal rebirths from theft and sin.)

[^1]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 44](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/).

[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.14](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-10f0fc).

[^3]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.15](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-3e2aab).

[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.18](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-a135b8).

[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.20](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-0bb922).

[^6]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.21](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-6e9baf).

[^7]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.24](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-70946b).

[^8]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.26](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-6d177e).

[^9]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Vi_44.29](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/44/translation/#p-079180).

[^10]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 12](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e).
